title: Invalid licence in commercial vehicle case – claimant-side research
query: Find precedents supporting a claimant where the insurer denies liability because a commercial vehicle driver lacked a valid licence or required endorsement. Also identify adverse or mixed cases the insurer may rely on.
expected_principles: insurer must satisfy third-party award despite licence defect in many third-party cases
pay-and-recover may be directed against the insured
absence of a separate endorsement for some commercial vehicles does not automatically exonerate the insurer
insurer bears burden to prove fundamental or wilful breach
expected_risk_themes: recovery rights for insurer against owner/insured
difference between third-party claims and own-damage/consumer disputes
notes: doc_001 is mixed rather than purely supportive because the insurer remains liable to claimants but gets recovery rights due to hazardous-goods endorsement defect.

title: Pay-and-recover doctrine – broad benchmark
query: Find precedents on when courts apply pay-and-recover after breach of policy conditions, and identify cases where courts refused to apply pay-and-recover.
expected_principles: pay-and-recover commonly protects third-party victims despite policy breach
courts may refuse pay-and-recover where the claimant is outside statutory/policy coverage
Article 142 style directions cannot be assumed as general statutory power for tribunals
breach must be analysed against the statutory structure of Sections 147 and 149
expected_risk_themes: non-covered passengers
mudguard/unauthorised occupant claims
difference between discretionary equitable relief and statutory liability
notes: This task is ideal for adverse-identification scoring because the corpus contains both strong pro-claimant pay-and-recover cases and strong anti-claimant/non-coverage cases.

title: LMV licence vs commercial endorsement
query: Research whether the insurer can avoid liability merely because the driver had an LMV licence but lacked a separate commercial or badge endorsement.
expected_principles: absence of separate commercial endorsement on LMV licence may not by itself defeat third-party claim
some courts still treat missing transport badge/authorisation as breach but apply pay-and-recover
vehicle class and specific endorsement requirements matter
expected_risk_themes: jurisdiction-specific rule treatment
difference between lack of endorsement and total absence of licence
notes: doc_032 is the key supportive Supreme Court authority; doc_035 is adverse/more insurer-friendly on badge requirement; doc_001 is a tanker/hazardous-goods mixed authority.

title: Hazardous goods endorsement for tanker drivers
query: Find precedents dealing with special endorsement requirements for drivers of tankers or vehicles carrying hazardous goods, and explain whether claimants still recover against the insurer.
expected_principles: special endorsement may be required for hazardous goods vehicles
insurer may still have to satisfy third-party claim and recover later
not every endorsement defect leads to complete exoneration
expected_risk_themes: fine distinction between transport-vehicle competency and hazardous-goods endorsement
mixed authorities that help claimants on payment but help insurers on recovery
notes: This is intentionally a mixed-authority task; a good agent should avoid overstating any case as purely supportive.

title: Gratuitous/unauthorised passengers – adverse precedent benchmark
query: Find adverse precedents where claimants failed or the insurer was exonerated because the victim was a gratuitous or unauthorised passenger in a goods vehicle, tractor, or private hired vehicle.
expected_principles: Act-only policies do not automatically cover gratuitous passengers
pay-and-recover is not always available where the claimant is outside statutory coverage
some courts still direct pay-and-recover on equitable/discretionary grounds
expected_risk_themes: difference between true statutory third party and non-covered passenger
tractor mudguard and hired-private-vehicle passenger cases are especially risky for claimants
notes: This query is purpose-built for Dimension 4 because the system should surface insurer-favourable cases, not just claimant-friendly pay-and-recover authorities.

title: Contributory negligence
query: Find precedents on contributory negligence in motor accident claims, especially where the insurer or respondent argues the victim also contributed to the accident.
expected_principles: contributory negligence needs evidentiary foundation and cannot be assumed from an unproved site plan alone
tribunal may reduce compensation proportionately where claimant conduct contributes to accident
res ipsa / surrounding facts may defeat contributory-negligence defence
expected_risk_themes: lack of helmet/licence by victim may support deduction
site plan arguments can overstate contributory negligence if not proved
notes: doc_006 is claimant-supportive on rejecting weak contributory-negligence arguments; doc_009 is adverse because it imposes a 50% deduction.

title: Income proof, minimum wages, and documentary evidence
query: Find precedents on how courts assess income for compensation when documentary proof is weak or disputed, including use of minimum wages, DC rates, or ITRs.
expected_principles: minimum wages may be used when income is not proved
ITRs and stronger documentary evidence can displace rough notional approaches
courts need not rigidly confine themselves to one income benchmark if the record supports a different figure
expected_risk_themes: income inflation by unsupported oral claims
tension between beneficial legislation and proof discipline
notes: doc_001 is especially useful because it discusses DC rates, minimum wages, and ITR-based reassessment in connected matters.

title: Future prospects and multiplier in death cases
query: Find precedents on applying future prospects and selecting the correct multiplier in motor accident death claims.
expected_principles: future prospects depend on age and employment category
multiplier is selected with reference to age under Sarla Verma/Pranay Sethi style rules
number of actual dependents affects deductions and therefore final quantum
expected_risk_themes: non-dependent legal representatives may reduce dependency component
tribunals sometimes omit future prospects and require appellate correction
notes: doc_007 is adverse on dependency because it excludes a non-dependent son from the dependency calculation.

title: Child death compensation
query: Find precedents on compensation methodology where the deceased is a child in a motor accident claim.
expected_principles: use of notional income with inflation/CII adjustment in child death cases
multiplier and non-pecuniary components are handled through a specialized formula
criminal-record evidence plus claimant testimony may suffice to prove negligence on preponderance
expected_risk_themes: child compensation is formula-sensitive and easy for an agent to misstate
notes: A good system should cite both Delhi MACT-style child death authorities here rather than only general death-compensation cases.

title: Permanent disability and future medical expenses
query: Find precedents on compensation in serious injury cases involving permanent disability, loss of earning capacity, and future medical expenses.
expected_principles: functional disability is not always equal to medical disability percentage
future prospects may apply in severe injury cases affecting earning capacity
future medical and attendant charges may be recoverable
expected_risk_themes: agents often confuse medical impairment with economic disability
notes: doc_020 is the key benchmark case for functional disability and future medicals; doc_024 adds a spinal-injury/pay-and-recover variant.

title: Commercial vehicles with insurer-contested liability – corpus Q&A
query: Which judgments in this corpus involve commercial vehicles and contested insurer liability?
expected_risk_themes: metadata filtering should be accurate; false positives indicate weak extraction or filter logic
notes: This task is mainly for checking metadata-aware retrieval and precision on a general corpus query rather than legal synthesis.

title: Owner-use/policy-use breach in tractor or attached trolley cases
query: Find precedents where insurer liability turned on the nature of vehicle use, especially tractors, attached trolleys, or vehicles used outside the policy's permitted purpose.
expected_principles: tractor/trolley coverage can depend on whether the use remained within insured/agricultural purpose
unauthorised carriage or use outside policy purpose may exonerate insurer
tractor mudguard occupant cases are especially insurer-favourable
expected_risk_themes: agents may over-generalize pay-and-recover into non-covered use cases
notes: This task complements the gratuitous-passenger benchmark by focusing on use/purpose breach rather than only passenger status.
